We have rovers with equipment and stores. Need to collect soil samples at waypoints 14 and 0, rock samples at 4,7,2,5,6, and take images of objectives 5 colour, 3 low_res, 2 colour, 4 high_res, then communicate all data to lander (general) at waypoint17. Need channel free (initially free). Need rovers to be available (they are initially). After communication, available again.

We must respect energy constraints and recharges minimized. Energy start 50 each rover. Navigation costs 8 each move. Sampling costs 3 (soil) or 5 (rock). Calibration costs 2. Imaging costs 1. Communication costs 4 (soil/rock) or 6 (image). Recharge adds 20 if energy <=80 and at sunny waypoint.

Goal: minimize recharges; likely zero recharges needed if we manage energy.

We have many rovers; we can assign tasks to each.

We need to know which rovers have which equipment:

- rover0: equipped for soil, rock, imaging. Has camera5 (on_board camera5 rover0) with calibration_target objective2, supports colour. So camera5 can take images of objective2 (colour mode). So to take image of objective2, need calibrate camera5 with objective2, then take_image with mode colour (supported). That would produce have_image rover0 objective2 colour. Then communicate_image_data objective2 colour.

Goal includes objective2 colour im

t14). Use communicate_soil_data rover0 general waypoint0 waypoint14 waypoint0? Wait parameters: (?r - rover ?l - lander ?p - waypoint ?x - waypoint ?y - waypoint). Now rock samples: need rock at waypoints 4,7,2,5,6.
